rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement Regarding Post-Trial Issues In The Civil Trial Under Related Case No. Cgc-22-599976; Request For Retention Of Jurisdiction Under Ccp 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The Court to determine how to proceed. a) This is highly irregular, as the Probate Division had no part in the Civil matter. b) The settlement agreement itself provided for specific dates by when this petition would be filed and heard, when hearing dates are set by the clerk, not by petitioners. c) Furthermore, the hearing dates agreed to by the settling parties, "March 6, 7 or 8, 2024" for a petition to be filed "on February 28, 2024," would not have met any statutory notice period. d) No support is given for the allegations regarding the sums each party claimed for the post-trial costs and attorney fees, etc., for this Court to determine if this settlement is, indeed, in the best interest of the estate. e) The petition does not appear to meet all the requirements of LRSF 14.43 D. A petition to approve the settlement must include the following: 1) A summary of the main terms of the settlement; 2) A citation to the Probate Code section under which the petition is presented; 3) Application of the relevant substantive legal standard for approval to the facts presented in the petition showing that the legal standard has been met; 4) A list of the persons entitled to notice of the petition; and, 5) A copy of the settlement agreement. 2) Defective Notice: a) Proof of service is incomplete at item 2. b) The pleading was not e-filed until 3/1/24. Notice was mailed on 2/28/24. [LRSF 14.14 A.] Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTORIA C. DRAYTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Entitlement To Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (see tentative rulings for the past two hearings) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Please confirm whether there was any mortgage on the estate real property at the time of the predeceased spouse's death. 4) Has the estate real property been sold? If so, please state the amount of cash currently held by the estate. 5) All the persons having legal custody of and residing with Ean Foster (minor) must be identified and receive a separate copy of notice. Direct notice must also be given to the minor. CRC 7.51(d). 6) Patrice Green's address listed on the proof of service is slightly different from the address set forth on Exhibit A attached to the petition. 7) A proof must be filed to establish a parent-child relationship as to each unidentified child of Henry Lucas and Peter Drayton. 8) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed to show efforts made to locate each unidentified child of Henry Lucas and Peter Drayton. Whether notice can be prescribed in any manner other than mailing (telephone, e-mail, social media, etc.) must also be addressed. CRC 7.52(c). 9) Please explain why no share is allocated to each unidentified child of Peter Drayton. Note: Mecca Thompson's objection on file states that Alice Lucas died without issue and disputes the proposed distribution of 1/280 (= 1/2 x 1/10 x 1/14) to Sabrina Williams and Dominic Williams as the purported surviving issue of Alice Lucas. The objection also states that Henry Lucas left no surviving issue and disputes the proposed distribution of 1/220 (= 1/2 x 1/10 x 1/11) to Henry Lucas' unidentified child. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBERT A. STEPICEVICH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/9/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 2/20/24 shows that notice was of the "First supplement to amended petition," etc., not of the amended petition. (A supplement does not generally require notice.) (2) The financial statements attached to the supplement filed on 3/8/24 did NOT verify the assets on hand as of 10/31/23. Nearly all of the brokered CDs have changed as of 12/31/23. (3) If brokered CDs can lose value when they mature, then assets on hand were NOT all cash, as alleged on Exhibit B (proposed distribution). If all CDs have "now" (when?) been cashed out, what is the current balance of cash on hand and as of what date? [See LRSF 14.35 I.6.d. and CRC 7.651(b).] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/29/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The allowances of the creditor claims were filed on 2/20/24, not 2/20/22. The Court is well aware that the value of assets changes during administration, but a personal representative's responsibility is to preserve assets to the extent possible and to maintain most of the cash in interest-bearing accounts, so that receipts of distribution will be exactly as ordered, including specific shares of securities if securities are still on hand, and slightly higher amounts cash.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LIONEL WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Establishing Trust'S Claim Of Ownership Of Property And For Authorizing And Directing Transfer To Terrellynn Westtaylor, Trustee Of The Lionel West Trust Established September 16, 2014, Amended February 22, 2016 (Probate Code 850(A)(3)(B)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/30/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) How is this the proper venue for this trust petition, which was filed in a decedentâs estate case, not a new trust case? The trustee presumably administers the trust in Maryland, where she resides. (2) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. (3) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (4) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (5) The copy of the trust attached as Exhibit A was not actually signed and dated by the settlor and trustee. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/19/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HELENE RENE GLIEBERMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/30/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The only correct form of notice at this stage (opening estate administration) is the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate, which must clearly indicate that an amended petition has been filed. (2) Published notice must also clearly indicate that an amended petition has been filed. (3) Bond was waived only for the executors under the will, not for any proposed administrator with will annexed. Waivers of bond must be filed or the full amount of bond will be required. (4) The amended petition still does not show the dates of death of "deceased" persons. (An amended pleading means a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC. 7.3.]) The attorney's "supplement to clear probate notes" was (a) unnecessary as an amended petition and other papers were filed that were meant to address issues posted in the tentative ruling and (b) was not signed and verified by the proposed personal representative. [CRC 7.103(c).] (5) Item 9 of the amended petition is (still) incomplete. (6) The will attestation is incomplete: the witnesses did not date it properly. (7) If the petitioner resides in Sacramento, why does she have a Michigan driver's license? (8) Gliberman or Glieberman? Deadline is 4/19/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues: (1) the petitioner's own supplement to her amended petition; (2) proof of service of Notice of Amended Petition, etc.; (3) proof of publication of the Amended Petition. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Please do not file an "answer sheet" to these issues. A supplement or an amendment to a pleading should stand on its own and merely provide additional information or correct information, as the case may be.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE HATIE LEE JOHNSON REVOCABLE TRUST DATED SEPTEMber 7, 2012, an irrevocable non testementary
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Disinherit And/Or Disqualify Respondent Trust Benificiary And Alternative Trustee Pursuant To California Probate Code Section 21311; Pargr
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petitioning trustee resides in Nevada. In May 2023, the respondent opened a trust case under the name of this trust and filed a petition to seek relief against the trustee. The Nevada court does not appear to have declined to exercise jurisdiction over the trust in favor of any court in California as a suitable forum. To the contrary, the Nevada court appears to have already issued certain orders, including an order requiring the trustee to deposit in a blocked account the proceeds from the sale of the trust real property located in San Francisco. CCP section 410.30 provides that: "When a court upon motion of a party or its own motion finds that in the interest of substantial justice an action should be heard in a forum outside this state, the court shall stay or dismiss the action in whole or in part on any conditions that may be just." Probate Code section 17202 provides that: "The court may dismiss a petition if it appears that the proceeding is not reasonably necessary for the protection of the interests of the trustee or beneficiary." 2) The petition does not allege that, as of the date of the filing of this petition in December 2023, after the trust real property in San Francisco had been sold, the trustee was carrying on the day-to-day activity of the trust in San Francisco, nor does the petition allege that the trustee's residence or usual place of business was in San Francisco. See sections 17002 & 17005(a)(1). If the principal place of administration of the trust is in Nevada where the trustee resides, the proper venue is determined by the rules applicable to civil actions. See section 17005(c); see also Law Revision Commission Comments to section 17005 ("when the principal place of administration of a trust is in another state, but jurisdiction is proper in California, the general rules governing venue apply"). CCP section 395(a) provides in relevant part that "the superior court in the county where the defendants or some of them reside at the commencement of the action is the proper court for the trial of the action". The respondent resides in San Pablo, Contra Costa County. 3) Section 21310(b)(6) defines a direct contest to include an allegation of a donative transfer to a disqualified beneficiary under former section 21350 or 21380, but disqualification does not apply to a beneficiary who is related by blood within the fourth degree to the transferor. See section 21382(a). The respondent's Nevada pleading does not appear to have challenged the validity of the trust on any ground listed in section 21310(b)(1)-(5) or paragraph 7.4(a)-(e) of the trust. Even if the respondent's Nevada pleading requesting an order to deposit the sale proceeds in a blocked account and claiming compensation and expenses for the respondent's caregiver services to the settlor constitutes indirect contests under section 21311(a)(2) and (3), the no-contest clause (paragraph 7.4 of the trust) did not expressly provide for application of those indirect contests, as required by each subdivision. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUDITH LENORE GARNIER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/09/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 02/05/2024 - see also tentative ruling for 02/27/2024 hearing) remaiin unaddressed: 1) No original will has been filed. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) No DE-147 form is on file. 4) No DE-147S form is on file. A pleading cover sheet must be attached to this form. 5) No DE-121 form is on file. The words "second amended" (petition) must be added where applicable on the form. A copy of the remote appearance information required by Local Rule of Court (LRSF) 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 6) No proof of publication is on file. The words "second amended" (petition) must be added where applicable in the published notice. 7) The box in the caption of the second amended petition to request IAEA authority was not checked. 8) Re item 3d of the second amended petition: i) Is the estate property the decedent's separate property, her one-half share of community property, or both? ii) Does the decedent's surviving spouse intend to make an election under section 13502(a) to probate his share of the estate otherwise passing to him without probate administration, in lieu of proceeding with a spousal property petition? 9) The will is not self-proving because the attestation clause contained in the will failed to qualify as an affidavit under Probate Code section 8220(b) and CCP section 2015.5. A proof of subscribing witness (DE-131 form) must be filed. 10) Re Ali Garnier: i) Please state his date of birth. ii) All persons having legal custody of and residing with him (e.g., both parents) must be identified and receive a separate copy of notice. CRC 7.51(d). 11) Item 9 of the second amended petition must specify the number of attachment pages. Note: An appearance may be required on 04/09/2024 for the Court to determine whether to take this petition off calendar, unless papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RICHARD P. GRANZELLA TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To 1) Invalidate Amendment (Prob Code 6104 And 811); 2) Remove Trustee; 3) Return Trust Property Prob Code 850 And 859; 4) Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/23/2024. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 3) Are all the beneficiaries listed in paragraph 16 of the petition adults? 4) According to Exhibit F (trustee notification under Probate Code section 16061.7), the principal place of administration of the trust is located in San Mateo County. Should this petition have been filed in San Mateo County?
